3.description: Parallel trend of hospital financial conditions This figure plots the dynamic effects of telehealth adoption on hospital financial conditions over a 10-year window. We follow the method in Equation (1), except that we seperate treatment effects into calendar year relative to the year a state adopts a telehealth parity law and estimate the effects on the yearly hospital sample. Circles represent annual estimated coefficients, and dashed lines indicate the 95% confidence interval.

4.description: Dynamic effects of telehealth adoption on cost of capital in the secondary market This figure plots effects of telehealth adoption on costs of capital before and after the shock using Equation (3) and the interaction weighted (IW) estimator from Sun and Abraham (2021). Circles represent the annual estimated coefficients βn, and dashed lines indicate the 95% confidence interval. In panel A, we use Yieldi,t as the outcome variable to estimate Equation (3) in the full sample. In panel B, we replicate the analysis in the sample of parity states. We follow Sun and Abraham (2021) and plot the IW estimator in each event period using either nonparity states (panel C) or the last treated rural hospitals (panel D) as the control group. In the last two figures, we use Spreadi,t (panel E) and Spreadi,t (panel F) as the outcome variable for Equation (3).

5.description: Effects of telehealth adoption on hospital closure This figure plots effects of telehealth adoption on hospital closures, using the estimation from Table 7, column 1. The blue line plots the estimated cumulative closure probability with Ruralm×Parityj,t=1⁠; the green line plots with Ruralm=1 and Parityj,t=0⁠; and the red line plots the probability with Ruralm=0⁠. We use the mean values of other predictors for estimation.
